hypothesis: A pre-entry H1 liquidity sweep context reacts faster than matched non-sweep controls without worsening fast SL20 risk.
deterministic_formula: Using completed pre-anchor H1 candles, define a swing high/low as a two-left/two-right fractal that is fully formed before anchor. A sweep exists when completed pre-anchor M15/M5 candles trade beyond that swing by >=5 pips and then close back inside the level or stall within 5 pips before anchor.
expected_signal_direction: Reversal against the swept side; upward sweep implies SHORT context, downward sweep implies LONG context.
candidate_window_definition: Anchor is the first completed M5 candle at least 5 minutes after the pre-anchor sweep condition is satisfied; no forward movement may be consulted.
control_window_definition: Random windows in the same date range/session/volatility bucket without the locked H1 sweep condition, using the same anchor spacing rules.
matching_controls: Match controls by session, volatility bucket, time-of-day, month or temporal block, source/context class where available, and avoid same-window contamination.
temporal_split_rule: Use an anchored temporal split: oldest 60% design/calibration-free diagnostic, next 20% validation, newest 20% OOS; do not change formulas after any outcome review.
oos_rule: OOS must use the locked formula, locked control matching, and locked metrics; lower N is INCONCLUSIVE, never PASS.
pass_criteria: PASS only if candidate fast_reaction_rate >= control + 0.07 and candidate fast_sl20_rate is not worse than control by more than 0.03 with n>=300 for serious validation.
fail_criteria: FAIL if candidate-control difference is within +/-3pp on the primary metric, or if the effect is only visible after unregistered subgrouping.
kill_criteria: KILL if fast failure worsens materially, formula requires future/unavailable data, effect exists only in one regime/session/month, OOS collapses, continuation-positive interpretation is required, or human signoff is missing.
anti_leakage_rules: Use only candles/timestamps closed before anchor; no post-entry movement, no future candle outcome, no HTF candle that has not closed, no chart cherry picking, no continuation-positive bias.
forbidden_interpretations: Continuation is not evidence of trade quality and must not be used as a positive entry reason.
notes: This is a sweep-context test, not a standalone Adelin entry rule.

hypothesis: A completed pre-anchor M5/M15 FVG near the candidate interaction improves reaction quality versus matched windows without such an FVG.
deterministic_formula: Bullish FVG: low(C3) > high(C1). Bearish FVG: high(C3) < low(C1). Use only completed pre-anchor candles; minimum gap size is 5 pips; record zone boundaries and mitigation state before anchor.
expected_signal_direction: Contextual reversal toward the side implied by the reaction zone; direction must be supplied by a separately registered candidate definition in a future execution branch.
candidate_window_definition: Anchor is the first completed M5 candle after price interacts with a pre-anchor FVG zone under the locked formula; no post-anchor confirmation is allowed.
control_window_definition: Matched windows in the same session/volatility/month without an eligible FVG interaction before anchor.
matching_controls: Match controls by session, volatility bucket, time-of-day, month or temporal block, source/context class where available, and avoid same-window contamination.
temporal_split_rule: Use an anchored temporal split: oldest 60% design/calibration-free diagnostic, next 20% validation, newest 20% OOS; do not change formulas after any outcome review.
oos_rule: OOS must use the locked formula, locked control matching, and locked metrics; lower N is INCONCLUSIVE, never PASS.
pass_criteria: PASS only if candidate fast_reaction_rate >= control + 0.07 or candidate fast_sl20_rate <= control - 0.10, with no OOS collapse.
fail_criteria: FAIL if candidate-control difference is within +/-3pp on the primary metric, or if the effect is only visible after unregistered subgrouping.
kill_criteria: KILL if fast failure worsens materially, formula requires future/unavailable data, effect exists only in one regime/session/month, OOS collapses, continuation-positive interpretation is required, or human signoff is missing.
anti_leakage_rules: Use only candles/timestamps closed before anchor; no post-entry movement, no future candle outcome, no HTF candle that has not closed, no chart cherry picking, no continuation-positive bias.
forbidden_interpretations: Continuation is not evidence of trade quality and must not be used as a positive entry reason.
notes: FVG is measurable, but mitigation thresholds must be locked before execution.

hypothesis: A completed pre-anchor IFVG retest is associated with better immediate reaction than matched controls.
deterministic_formula: Start from the locked FVG formula. IFVG requires the FVG to be mitigated by completed pre-anchor candles and retested from the opposite side by the last completed pre-anchor M5/M15 candle; anchor candle retests are invalid.
expected_signal_direction: Reversal from the retested inverse zone; direction must be deterministic and pre-anchor in the future execution branch.
candidate_window_definition: Anchor is after the completed pre-anchor retest candle; the anchor candle itself cannot create or validate the retest.
control_window_definition: Matched windows with no eligible pre-anchor IFVG retest, preserving session/volatility/month distribution.
matching_controls: Match controls by session, volatility bucket, time-of-day, month or temporal block, source/context class where available, and avoid same-window contamination.
temporal_split_rule: Use an anchored temporal split: oldest 60% design/calibration-free diagnostic, next 20% validation, newest 20% OOS; do not change formulas after any outcome review.
oos_rule: OOS must use the locked formula, locked control matching, and locked metrics; lower N is INCONCLUSIVE, never PASS.
pass_criteria: PASS only if candidate fast_reaction_rate >= control + 0.07 and OOS fast_sl20_rate is not worse than control.
fail_criteria: FAIL if candidate-control difference is within +/-3pp on the primary metric, or if the effect is only visible after unregistered subgrouping.
kill_criteria: KILL if fast failure worsens materially, formula requires future/unavailable data, effect exists only in one regime/session/month, OOS collapses, continuation-positive interpretation is required, or human signoff is missing.
anti_leakage_rules: Use only candles/timestamps closed before anchor; no post-entry movement, no future candle outcome, no HTF candle that has not closed, no chart cherry picking, no continuation-positive bias.
forbidden_interpretations: Continuation is not evidence of trade quality and must not be used as a positive entry reason.
notes: IFVG retest is higher leakage risk than simple FVG because retest timing must exclude anchor and post-anchor candles.

hypothesis: Candidate contexts whose swept or reaction level is near the frozen numeric-level grid show lower fast-failure risk than matched non-confluent contexts.
deterministic_formula: Frozen grid: XAUUSD price levels divisible by 10.00 USD, for example 4830.00, 4900.00, and 4910.00. pip_factor = 10, so pips / 10 = USD and 20 pips = 2.0 USD. For a pre-anchor swept or reaction level, NUMERIC_LEVEL_CONFLUENCE is true only when distance to the nearest frozen grid level is <=20 pips (<=2.0 USD). The swept/reaction level must be known before anchor.
expected_signal_direction: Confluence only; no standalone trade direction is allowed from number theory.
candidate_window_definition: Anchor windows whose pre-anchor swept or reaction level is within <=20 pips (<=2.0 USD) of the frozen XAUUSD numeric grid divisible by 10.00 USD.
control_window_definition: Matched windows with pre-anchor swept or reaction levels outside the <=20 pip numeric-level threshold, otherwise comparable by session, volatility, and temporal block.
matching_controls: Match controls by session, volatility bucket, time-of-day, month or temporal block, source/context class where available, and avoid same-window contamination.
temporal_split_rule: Use an anchored temporal split: oldest 60% design/calibration-free diagnostic, next 20% validation, newest 20% OOS; do not change formulas after any outcome review.
oos_rule: OOS must use the locked formula, locked control matching, and locked metrics; lower N is INCONCLUSIVE, never PASS.
pass_criteria: PASS only if candidate fast_sl20_rate <= control - 0.10 and fast_reaction_rate is not worse than control by more than 0.03.
fail_criteria: FAIL if candidate-control difference is within +/-3pp on the primary metric, or if the effect is only visible after unregistered subgrouping.
kill_criteria: KILL if fast failure worsens materially, formula requires future/unavailable data, effect exists only in one regime/session/month, OOS collapses, continuation-positive interpretation is required, or human signoff is missing.
anti_leakage_rules: Use only candles/timestamps closed before anchor; no post-entry movement, no future candle outcome, no HTF candle that has not closed, no chart cherry picking, no continuation-positive bias.
forbidden_interpretations: Continuation is not evidence of trade quality and must not be used as a positive entry reason.
notes: This is the only primary numeric-level proximity hypothesis. Spec 005 is a stratification of this test, not an independent primary feature.

hypothesis: Stratify the 004 NUMERIC_LEVEL_CONFLUENCE context by distance band to check whether tighter proximity changes descriptive behavior; this is metadata only, not an independent signal hypothesis.
deterministic_formula: Use the same frozen grid as spec 004: XAUUSD price levels divisible by 10.00 USD. Compute distance from the pre-anchor swept or reaction level to the nearest spec-004 grid level with pip_factor = 10. Assign bands: 0-10 pips (0.0-1.0 USD, inclusive), 10-20 pips (>1.0-2.0 USD), and >20 pips (>2.0 USD).
expected_signal_direction: Stratification only; tight numeric-level distance does not infer LONG or SHORT and is not a standalone entry reason.
candidate_window_definition: No standalone candidate windows. Attach tight_numeric_level_touch_band metadata to spec-004 candidates and to any future pre-registered candidate/control rows.
control_window_definition: No standalone controls. Apply the same distance bands to matched controls from spec 004 or future locked candidate/control comparisons.
matching_controls: Match controls by session, volatility bucket, time-of-day, month or temporal block, source/context class where available, and avoid same-window contamination.
temporal_split_rule: Use an anchored temporal split: oldest 60% design/calibration-free diagnostic, next 20% validation, newest 20% OOS; do not change formulas after any outcome review.
oos_rule: OOS must use the locked formula, locked control matching, and locked metrics; lower N is INCONCLUSIVE, never PASS.
pass_criteria: No standalone PASS is allowed. This spec can only describe stratified behavior inside another locked primary test.
fail_criteria: FAIL as a primary hypothesis by design; any attempt to use this band as an independent signal invalidates the future test.
kill_criteria: KILL any future interpretation that treats tight_numeric_level_touch_band as an independent edge, standalone entry feature, or separate primary PASS criterion.
anti_leakage_rules: Use only candles/timestamps closed before anchor; no post-entry movement, no future candle outcome, no HTF candle that has not closed, no chart cherry picking, no continuation-positive bias.
forbidden_interpretations: Continuation is not evidence of trade quality and must not be used as a positive entry reason. tight_numeric_level_touch_band must not be interpreted as an independent edge or standalone entry feature.
notes: Spec 005 is a subtype/stratification of spec 004, not an independent feature test. It was removed from primary tests to fix the 004/005 overlap before human signoff.

hypothesis: Locked wick/body rejection morphology before anchor predicts lower fast-failure risk versus matched morphology-neutral controls.
deterministic_formula: On completed pre-anchor M1/M5 candles touching the candidate level, compute body/range, upper_wick/range, lower_wick/range, and close-location value. A rejection morphology flag is true only under locked side-specific thresholds recorded before execution.
expected_signal_direction: Side-specific reversal context only when paired with a separately registered direction source; morphology alone cannot infer entry direction.
candidate_window_definition: Anchor follows a completed pre-anchor candle that touches the candidate level and satisfies locked morphology thresholds.
control_window_definition: Matched windows touching comparable levels but not satisfying the locked morphology thresholds.
matching_controls: Match controls by session, volatility bucket, time-of-day, month or temporal block, source/context class where available, and avoid same-window contamination.
temporal_split_rule: Use an anchored temporal split: oldest 60% design/calibration-free diagnostic, next 20% validation, newest 20% OOS; do not change formulas after any outcome review.
oos_rule: OOS must use the locked formula, locked control matching, and locked metrics; lower N is INCONCLUSIVE, never PASS.
pass_criteria: PASS only if fast_sl20_rate <= control - 0.10 and reaction metrics do not degrade in OOS.
fail_criteria: FAIL if candidate-control difference is within +/-3pp on the primary metric, or if the effect is only visible after unregistered subgrouping.
kill_criteria: KILL if fast failure worsens materially, formula requires future/unavailable data, effect exists only in one regime/session/month, OOS collapses, continuation-positive interpretation is required, or human signoff is missing.
anti_leakage_rules: Use only candles/timestamps closed before anchor; no post-entry movement, no future candle outcome, no HTF candle that has not closed, no chart cherry picking, no continuation-positive bias.
forbidden_interpretations: Continuation is not evidence of trade quality and must not be used as a positive entry reason.
notes: Morphology is measurable but can overfit; thresholds must not be tuned from outcomes.

hypothesis: Pre-anchor displacement near a candidate level improves immediate reaction quality versus matched controls.
deterministic_formula: Using completed pre-anchor M1/M5 candles, displacement is true when candle range is >=2.0 times rolling median range of the prior 20 completed candles and body/range >=0.65. Thresholds are locked before execution.
expected_signal_direction: Displacement direction can describe context but cannot create a standalone entry; it must be tested as metadata around a separate candidate definition.
candidate_window_definition: Anchor follows a completed pre-anchor displacement candle or sequence near the candidate level under the locked formula.
control_window_definition: Matched windows without displacement under the locked formula but comparable by session/volatility/month.
matching_controls: Match controls by session, volatility bucket, time-of-day, month or temporal block, source/context class where available, and avoid same-window contamination.
temporal_split_rule: Use an anchored temporal split: oldest 60% design/calibration-free diagnostic, next 20% validation, newest 20% OOS; do not change formulas after any outcome review.
oos_rule: OOS must use the locked formula, locked control matching, and locked metrics; lower N is INCONCLUSIVE, never PASS.
pass_criteria: PASS only if fast_reaction_rate >= control + 0.07 and fast_sl20_rate is not worse than control by more than 0.03.
fail_criteria: FAIL if candidate-control difference is within +/-3pp on the primary metric, or if the effect is only visible after unregistered subgrouping.
kill_criteria: KILL if fast failure worsens materially, formula requires future/unavailable data, effect exists only in one regime/session/month, OOS collapses, continuation-positive interpretation is required, or human signoff is missing.
anti_leakage_rules: Use only candles/timestamps closed before anchor; no post-entry movement, no future candle outcome, no HTF candle that has not closed, no chart cherry picking, no continuation-positive bias.
forbidden_interpretations: Continuation is not evidence of trade quality and must not be used as a positive entry reason.
notes: News-like large candles must be tagged later, not used to tune this feature.

hypothesis: Locked time-of-day buckets have materially different reaction/failure profiles and should be used for stratification before any feature-set candidate.
deterministic_formula: Convert anchor timestamp to UTC and assign immutable hour/minute buckets before outcome replay. No price movement is used in this feature.
expected_signal_direction: Stratification only; time-of-day cannot be a positive standalone entry reason.
candidate_window_definition: All future candidate windows are tagged with locked time-of-day buckets before replay.
control_window_definition: Controls must match time-of-day bucket or be reported as unmatched and excluded from PASS decisions.
matching_controls: Match controls by session, volatility bucket, time-of-day, month or temporal block, source/context class where available, and avoid same-window contamination.
temporal_split_rule: Use an anchored temporal split: oldest 60% design/calibration-free diagnostic, next 20% validation, newest 20% OOS; do not change formulas after any outcome review.
oos_rule: OOS must use the locked formula, locked control matching, and locked metrics; lower N is INCONCLUSIVE, never PASS.
pass_criteria: PASS only as a control-quality gate: candidate effects must remain directionally consistent across time buckets with sufficient N.
fail_criteria: FAIL if candidate-control difference is within +/-3pp on the primary metric, or if the effect is only visible after unregistered subgrouping.
kill_criteria: KILL if fast failure worsens materially, formula requires future/unavailable data, effect exists only in one regime/session/month, OOS collapses, continuation-positive interpretation is required, or human signoff is missing.
anti_leakage_rules: Use only candles/timestamps closed before anchor; no post-entry movement, no future candle outcome, no HTF candle that has not closed, no chart cherry picking, no continuation-positive bias.
forbidden_interpretations: Continuation is not evidence of trade quality and must not be used as a positive entry reason.
notes: This is a matching/stratification feature, not a signal feature.

hypothesis: Session buckets explain enough XAUUSD behavior that every future candidate/control comparison must session-match before any PASS decision.
deterministic_formula: Convert anchor timestamp to UTC and assign locked buckets: ASIA, ASIA_OPEN, LONDON, LONDON_OPEN, NEW_YORK, NEW_YORK_OPEN, OTHER. DST and broker-time handling must be fixed before execution.
expected_signal_direction: Stratification only; session cannot be a standalone positive entry reason.
candidate_window_definition: All future candidate windows carry a pre-anchor session bucket from timestamp only.
control_window_definition: Controls must be session-matched by default; unmatched controls are debug-only and excluded from PASS decisions.
matching_controls: Match controls by session, volatility bucket, time-of-day, month or temporal block, source/context class where available, and avoid same-window contamination.
temporal_split_rule: Use an anchored temporal split: oldest 60% design/calibration-free diagnostic, next 20% validation, newest 20% OOS; do not change formulas after any outcome review.
oos_rule: OOS must use the locked formula, locked control matching, and locked metrics; lower N is INCONCLUSIVE, never PASS.
pass_criteria: PASS only as a baseline-quality gate: any candidate edge must survive session-matched controls and avoid single-session collapse.
fail_criteria: FAIL if candidate-control difference is within +/-3pp on the primary metric, or if the effect is only visible after unregistered subgrouping.
kill_criteria: KILL if fast failure worsens materially, formula requires future/unavailable data, effect exists only in one regime/session/month, OOS collapses, continuation-positive interpretation is required, or human signoff is missing.
anti_leakage_rules: Use only candles/timestamps closed before anchor; no post-entry movement, no future candle outcome, no HTF candle that has not closed, no chart cherry picking, no continuation-positive bias.
forbidden_interpretations: Continuation is not evidence of trade quality and must not be used as a positive entry reason.
notes: Session context is mandatory matching metadata.

hypothesis: Pre-anchor volatility buckets determine whether candidate effects are stable or regime-specific.
deterministic_formula: Compute ATR/range percentile from completed pre-anchor D1/H1/M15 candles only. Assign LOW/MID/HIGH buckets from locked historical quantiles computed without using post-anchor outcome.
expected_signal_direction: Stratification/risk context only; volatility bucket cannot be a standalone positive entry reason.
candidate_window_definition: Every future candidate window must carry a pre-anchor volatility bucket before replay.
control_window_definition: Controls must match volatility bucket where possible; unmatched controls are excluded from PASS decisions unless explicitly pre-approved as diagnostic-only.
matching_controls: Match controls by session, volatility bucket, time-of-day, month or temporal block, source/context class where available, and avoid same-window contamination.
temporal_split_rule: Use an anchored temporal split: oldest 60% design/calibration-free diagnostic, next 20% validation, newest 20% OOS; do not change formulas after any outcome review.
oos_rule: OOS must use the locked formula, locked control matching, and locked metrics; lower N is INCONCLUSIVE, never PASS.
pass_criteria: PASS only as a stability gate: feature effects must not depend on a single volatility bucket and fast_sl20 must not worsen in any major bucket.
fail_criteria: FAIL if candidate-control difference is within +/-3pp on the primary metric, or if the effect is only visible after unregistered subgrouping.
kill_criteria: KILL if fast failure worsens materially, formula requires future/unavailable data, effect exists only in one regime/session/month, OOS collapses, continuation-positive interpretation is required, or human signoff is missing.
anti_leakage_rules: Use only candles/timestamps closed before anchor; no post-entry movement, no future candle outcome, no HTF candle that has not closed, no chart cherry picking, no continuation-positive bias.
forbidden_interpretations: Continuation is not evidence of trade quality and must not be used as a positive entry reason.
notes: Volatility regime is a mandatory stratification and kill-criteria feature.
